Package jsim.api

Class SimBody

java.lang.Object
jsim.api.SimBody
public final class SimBody extends Object
High-level handle to a simulated rigid body.

All reads and writes use WPILib geometry types. Physics internals are kept inside RigidBody and are not directly exposed.

Obtain instances from SimWorld.addBody(SimBodyBuilder).

  • Method Details

    • getId

      public int getId()
      Returns:
      world-assigned unique ID of the underlying physics body

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Returns:
      the name assigned to this body at creation time

    • getRobotId

      public RobotId getRobotId()
      The FRC alliance-station identity of this body, or null if it was not registered with SimBodyBuilder.robotId(RobotId).
      Returns:
      robot identity, or null

    • isRobot

      public boolean isRobot()
      true if this body was created with an alliance-station identity.
      Returns:
      true if a robot ID is set

    • getPose

      public edu.wpi.first.math.geometry.Pose3d getPose()
      Returns:
      current world-frame pose

    • getPosition

      public edu.wpi.first.math.geometry.Translation3d getPosition()
      Returns:
      current position in world frame (metres)

    • getRotation

      public edu.wpi.first.math.geometry.Rotation3d getRotation()
      Returns:
      current orientation in world frame

    • setPose

      public void setPose(edu.wpi.first.math.geometry.Pose3d pose)
      Teleport the body to a new pose (velocities are preserved).
      Parameters:
      pose - new world-frame pose

    • setPosition

      public void setPosition(double x, double y, double z)
      Teleport the body to a new position (rotation and velocities are preserved).
      Parameters:
      x - new X coordinate (metres)
      y - new Y coordinate (metres)
      z - new Z coordinate (metres)

    • getLinearVelocity

      public edu.wpi.first.math.geometry.Translation3d getLinearVelocity()
      Returns:
      linear velocity in world frame (m/s)

    • getAngularVelocity

      public edu.wpi.first.math.geometry.Translation3d getAngularVelocity()
      Returns:
      angular velocity in world frame (rad/s)

    • setLinearVelocity

      public void setLinearVelocity(edu.wpi.first.math.geometry.Translation3d v)
      Set the linear velocity directly from a Translation3d.
      Parameters:
      v - new linear velocity in world frame (m/s)

    • setAngularVelocity

      public void setAngularVelocity(edu.wpi.first.math.geometry.Translation3d omega)
      Set the angular velocity directly from a Translation3d.
      Parameters:
      omega - new angular velocity in world frame (rad/s)

    • setLinearVelocity

      public void setLinearVelocity(double vx, double vy, double vz)
      Set the linear velocity from scalar components.
      Parameters:
      vx - velocity X component (m/s)
      vy - velocity Y component (m/s)
      vz - velocity Z component (m/s)

    • setAngularVelocity

      public void setAngularVelocity(double omX, double omY, double omZ)
      Set the angular velocity from scalar components.
      Parameters:
      omX - angular velocity X component (rad/s)
      omY - angular velocity Y component (rad/s)
      omZ - angular velocity Z component (rad/s)

    • getSpeed

      public edu.wpi.first.units.measure.LinearVelocity getSpeed()
      Returns:
      scalar speed (magnitude of linear velocity)

    • getActuator

      public ActuatorForce getActuator()
      The actuator belonging to this body.

      Set forces/torques on it each robot loop iteration; they are picked up on the next physics tick.

      Returns:
      this body's actuator force generator

    • getMass

      public edu.wpi.first.units.measure.Mass getMass()
      Returns:
      mass of this body (POSITIVE_INFINITY for static bodies)

    • getMaterial

      public Material getMaterial()
      Returns:
      current surface material

    • setMaterial

      public void setMaterial(Material m)
      Sets the surface material used for contact response.
      Parameters:
      m - the new material

    • getCollider

      public ColliderShape getCollider()
      Returns:
      the collision shape attached to this body, or null

    • isStatic

      public boolean isStatic()
      Returns:
      true if this body has infinite mass and does not move

    • getAABB

      public double[] getAABB()
      Return the 6-element world-space AABB [minX,minY,minZ,maxX,maxY,maxZ] in metres.
      Returns:
      copy of the AABB array
